Petition To Amend Corrected Court Order Entered February 5, 2024, And To Reduce Liability On Bond
Off Calendar May-06-2024 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Oct-25-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Flaxman for Peta Banys (executor/petitioner).

The court stated that the clause regarding the 4% is kind of doing double duty. Mr. Flaxman stated that the executor was provided a gift, that at the end 4% was to be set aside for the executor and that separately in the will 4% is allowed for legal expenses which is permissible, valid and enforceable. The First and Final Account, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Off Calendar Apr-04-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-22-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Flaxman, via zoom, for Peta Geraldine Penson Banys, Petitioner.

The court and Mr. Flaxman discuss the venue issue with Mr. Flaxman reporting that venue is proper as Oppenheimer has offices in San Francisco. There is additional discussion regarding what assets make up the estate. Counsel reports that there is cash and some stocks and that there have been additional assets discovered and requests a bond in the amount of $10,000,000. The court recognizes the apostille authenticating the notary of the newly lodged will and confirms that the objection filed by Sue Herman has been withdrawn and that there are no appearances today to object. There being no objections, the Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated Jun-04-2015 is admitted to probate. Peta Geraldine Penson Banys is appointed Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $10,000,000. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Apr-04-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Maloney. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-01-2022 Continued To Mar-22-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-21-2021 Continued To Mar-01-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Continued To Dec-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Flaxman, via ZOOM, for Peta Banys, Petitioner; Sue Sherman, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Objector; C'Iaran O'Sullivan; via ZOOM, for Magda Moreno, Requestor.

The court advises Mr. Flaxman that there is an issue with venue. Mr. Flaxman reports that the Oppenheimer account is in San Francisco. The court advises Mr. Flaxman that Oppenheimer's principal place of business is in New York and so is the agent for process of service, so the court is not convinced that venue is proper and asks counsel to file a formal brief regarding the venue issue. Mr. Flaxman offers oral advocacy for his position regarding venue. The court points counsel to probate code 12521 regarding the issue of authentication and points counsel to evidence code 1530 and the specific requirements therein. The court and counsel discuss the amount of bond and advises the parties to meet and confer regarding the bond amount. The court advises Mr. Flaxman to focus on the venue and authentication issues and file briefs with the court.

The Petition for Probate of Will is continued to Dec-21-2021 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. Mr. Flaxman is instructed to file his briefs mentioned above by Dec-10-2021.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
